concerns.text: Base rate difference and class imbalance ratio are definitionally related (imbalance = base_rate/(1-base_rate)). Using both as independent predictors in regression violates predictor independence. This creates a circular validation where the outcome may be partially determined by the predictor.

Linear mixed-effects models with random intercepts AND random slopes require sufficient groups. With only ≥5 datasets, random effects estimation is statistically unreliable (minimum ~10-20 groups recommended). This undermines the validity of regression coefficients and p-values.

Bootstrap resampling at dataset level (n=1000) with only 5 datasets provides no meaningful uncertainty quantification. Resampling 5 items 1000 times does not reduce variance; consider bootstrap at observation level within datasets instead.

Some fairness metrics (Demographic Parity, Disparate Impact) are definitionally related to base rates. Correlating these metrics with base rate as a predictor creates circular validation where the outcome is partially determined by the predictor.

Bonferroni correction across 15 correlation tests with only 5 datasets is overly conservative (adjusted alpha ≈ 0.0033). This will likely produce Type II errors. Consider False Discovery Rate (FDR) control instead.
